findings.description: The Java pseudo-code block showing how the framework calls each template method is entirely omitted. This block provides the only source-level documentation of the method signatures: `onReceive(req, ctx)` (RequestMessage + ExecutionContext) and `onError(e, req, ctx)` (exception + RequestMessage + ExecutionContext). It also shows that `commit()` is called after onReceive and that `send(res)` is called in a finally block. These calling conventions are essential for implementing the template methods correctly.

`ExecutionContext` is referenced in the source code block as a parameter passed to `onReceive(req, ctx)` and `onError(e, req, ctx)`, making it a relevant class name for this section, but it is absent from s1 hints.
findings.source_evidence: ExecutionContext ctx; ... res = onReceive(req, ctx); ... res = onError(e, req, ctx);
